title: The Border at Dawn
subtitle: Guns, butter, and the price of safety.

turns.prompt: A hostile neighbour masses troops along your border. The capital wants reassurance. The world is watching.

choices.label: Mobilise the army and reinforce the border

delayed.headline: War spending starts crowding out everything else

choices.reaction: Tanks roll to the frontier. Markets shudder.

choices.label: Open quiet back-channel negotiations
choices.reaction: Envoys fly out at night. Hawks call it weakness.

choices.label: Call in foreign allies for protection
choices.reaction: Allied jets patrol your skies. Sovereignty feels smaller.

turns.prompt: The border holds. The crisis cools enough to think past the next week.

choices.label: Modernise the military for the long haul
choices.reaction: New hardware ordered. The bill is generational.

choices.label: Redirect the budget back to public services

delayed.headline: With defences thinned, provocations resume at the border

choices.reaction: Clinics reopen. Generals warn you'll regret it.

choices.label: Introduce national service
choices.reaction: The ranks swell. Families protest the draft.

turns.prompt: A skirmish at the frontier leaves soldiers dead. The country demands a response.

choices.label: Strike back hard to deter further attacks
choices.reaction: The enemy pauses. The whole region holds its breath.

choices.label: Sue for an unpopular ceasefire
choices.flavour: Swallow pride to stop the bleeding.
choices.reaction: The guns fall silent. So does national pride.

choices.label: Order full mobilisation for total war
choices.reaction: The nation girds itself. Normal life ends.

turns.prompt: Every earlier decision sends its invoice at once.

memory_callbacks.text: The strike you ordered pulled you into a war you can't easily exit.
Your protectors now expect a say in your every decision.
The defences you thinned left exactly the opening the enemy took.
Total mobilisation has bled the treasury white.

choices.label: Negotiate a costly peace
choices.flavour: End it, even if it hurts.
choices.reaction: Borders are redrawn. The maps look different now.

choices.label: Fund the fight with debt and patriotism
choices.reaction: Citizens buy war bonds. The future buys the bill.

choices.label: Dig in for a long defensive war
choices.flavour: Hold the line, whatever the cost.
choices.reaction: The line holds. The economy does not.

turns.prompt: The threat finally recedes. What did the long emergency make of your country?

choices.label: Demobilise and invest in peace
choices.reaction: Soldiers come home. The slow recovery begins.

choices.label: Keep the nation on permanent alert
choices.reaction: Safe, watchful, and never quite at ease.

choices.label: Convert the war economy to growth
choices.reaction: Factories retool for profit. Veterans feel forgotten.
